title: Add canonical goal write helpers to storage
goal: Make azq/finis/storage.py capable of writing one canonical goal record per file so other Finis modules can stop writing goals.json directly.
context: Wave A established the storage foundation, but storage.py still lacks write-side primitives.
The storage inspection report says storage.py feels like the right emerging authority but is not yet canonical because live code still bypasses it.
Wave B should make storage.py the real persistence boundary before refactoring the command modules.
requirements: Add a canonical single-record write helper such as save_goal() or upsert_goal().
Ensure the helper writes exactly one FINIS_###.md file under data/finis/goals/.
Ensure the helper uses the canonical normalized schema.
Keep the implementation small and readable.
acceptance_criteria: storage.py exposes a write-side API for one canonical goal record.
A caller can persist one normalized goal without touching goals.json.
The resulting goal file is canonical markdown under data/finis/goals/.
suggested_commit_message: add canonical finis goal write helper

title: Implement one-way legacy migration into canonical goal files
goal: Convert legacy goals.json records into canonical FINIS_###.md files without changing goal ids.
context: Stage 1 requires goals.json to become legacy migration input only.
The current data layer still has live goal data in goals.json and an empty data/finis/goals/ directory.
Migration should be additive and conservative.
requirements: Add a one-way migration function that reads goals.json through the legacy reader.
Normalize each record and write one FINIS_###.md file per goal.
Preserve existing goal_id values exactly.
Make migration idempotent enough to avoid duplicating already migrated files.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy JSON can be converted into canonical goal files.
Existing goal ids remain stable.
Repeated migration does not create duplicate goal files.
suggested_commit_message: add finis legacy to canonical goal migration

title: Choose and wire the migration trigger
goal: Make the system enter the canonical file-backed world deterministically before active Finis reads and writes continue.
context: The Stage 1 plan recommends one clear migration trigger, preferably automatic migration on first Finis read/write if it stays inspectable.
The active system must not silently continue writing only to goals.json.
requirements: Pick one migration trigger strategy and implement it cleanly.
Prefer the smallest approach that makes Stage 1 usable immediately.
If automatic, make the migration behavior explicit and inspectable.
Do not broaden into full command refactors yet.
acceptance_criteria: There is one deterministic migration path from legacy JSON to canonical goal files.
The chosen trigger runs before active Finis flows continue using legacy-only storage.
The trigger is inspectable and not magical.
suggested_commit_message: wire finis canonical storage migration trigger

title: Refactor azq goals into a thin presentation layer
goal: Turn azq/finis/goals.py into a read-only display module over the shared storage layer.
context: The goals.py inspection report recommends removing load_goals(), save_goals(), and next_goal_id() from goals.py.
show_goals() should remain, but it should read canonical goal records and display title instead of legacy goal.
requirements: Remove JSON-era storage ownership from goals.py.
Rewrite show_goals() to use azq.finis.storage.load_all_goals().
Display canonical title instead of legacy goal.
Preserve the distinction between 'no goals exist' and 'no active goals'.
acceptance_criteria: goals.py no longer owns storage helpers.
azq goals reads canonical records through storage.py.
show_goals() prints title and filters active goals only.
suggested_commit_message: refactor azq goals onto canonical storage layer

title: Refactor goal_manager into a thin command-layer writer
goal: Make azq/finis/goal_manager.py coordinate manual goal creation and status transitions while delegating persistence to storage.py.
context: The goal_manager.py inspection report says it should stop owning storage mechanics and become a thin command-layer coordinator.
The main remaining legacy write paths are add_goal(), close_goal(), and archive_goal().
requirements: Remove duplicated JSON-era storage helpers from goal_manager.py.
Rewrite add_goal() to create one canonical record using title, description, and derived_from.
Rewrite close_goal() to load one goal by exact id and rewrite only that canonical file with updated status.
Rewrite archive_goal() to load one goal by exact id and rewrite only that canonical file with status archived.
acceptance_criteria: goal_manager.py no longer reads or writes goals.json directly.
add_goal(), close_goal(), and archive_goal() operate on canonical file-backed records.
Status transitions use exact goal_id lookup.
suggested_commit_message: refactor goal manager onto canonical storage layer

title: Refactor fine onto canonical storage and title-based dedupe
goal: Make azq/finis/fine.py use canonical storage, canonical field names, and file-backed goal writes.
context: The fine.py inspection report says fine.py still imports legacy helpers from goals.py, dedupes against g['goal'], uses candidate objects with goal, and writes accepted goals back to goals.json.
This file is likely the easiest place for schema drift to survive if not migrated now.
requirements: Replace .goals imports with canonical storage-layer imports.
Change candidate dedupe to compare against canonical title values.
Change temporary candidate objects from goal to title.
Write accepted goals one file at a time as canonical records with description and derived_from.
acceptance_criteria: fine.py no longer depends on JSON-era helpers from goals.py.
Candidate dedupe compares against canonical title fields.
Accepted goals are written as canonical FINIS_###.md records, not to goals.json.
suggested_commit_message: refactor fine onto canonical goal storage

title: Remove duplicated JSON-era Finis helpers
goal: Delete the transitional helper functions that duplicate storage behavior now owned by azq/finis/storage.py.
context: Stage 1 explicitly calls for removing duplicated JSON persistence helpers once the shared storage layer is active.
The reports for goals.py, goal_manager.py, and fine.py all identify duplicated JSON-era helper paths as a drift risk.
requirements: Remove obsolete load_goals(), save_goals(), and next_goal_id() implementations from the legacy modules.
Remove no-longer-needed imports tied to JSON-era persistence.
Keep module responsibilities narrow and aligned with the Stage 1 architecture.
acceptance_criteria: There is one shared Finis storage authority.
The legacy modules no longer duplicate JSON-era persistence helpers.
The active Finis command path no longer writes new state to goals.json.
suggested_commit_message: remove duplicated legacy finis json helpers
